tool_input.summary: Located Cassini CIRS PDS3 volumes containing calibrated spectra and spectral image cubes suitable for tracking Titan’s post-2009-equinox polar temperature/composition structure, plus PDS4 derived Cassini RSS neutral-atmosphere T–P profile collection for Titan occultations and a PDS4 UVIS Titan spectral-library derived-data collection that can complement vortex/composition context.

candidates.title: CASSINI INFRARED SPECTRA
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1 (PDS3): Probed COCIRS_0909 and found DATA_SET_IDs including the *CIRS spectral image cubes* set (CO-S-CIRS-5-CUBES-V2.0). CIRS cubes are the most direct way on the ATM node to study the evolution of Titan’s north polar winter vortex after the 2009 equinox via time-series temperature and trace-gas retrievals and polar structure/mixing diagnostics.

candidates.title: CASSINI INFRARED SPECTRA
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1 (PDS3): Same probed volume (COCIRS_0909) also advertises the CIRS TSDR dataset (CO-S-CIRS-2/3/4-TSDR-V4.0), containing calibrated spectra/interferograms used to build the vortex diagnostics. This is the common cited PDS3 identifier for CIRS calibrated spectral data.

candidates.title: Cassini Orbiter RSS Neutral Atmosphere T-P profiles for Titan Derived Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1 (PDS4): Probed titan_profiles_bundle (bundle LID urn:nasa:pds:corsstpp) and inspected collections; the derived collection urn:nasa:pds:corsstpp:data_derived provides retrieved neutral-atmosphere temperature–pressure profiles from RSS occultations. These vertical profiles are a complementary way to characterize how Titan’s polar thermal structure changed as the northern winter vortex weakened after the 2009 equinox.

candidates.title: Cassini UVIS Titan Library Derived Data collection file
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1 (PDS4): Probed cassini-uvis_titan-library and inspected collections; urn:nasa:pds:cassini-uvis_titan-library:data_derived is a derived UVIS Titan spectral library. It can support vortex-evolution studies by providing complementary UV/occultation-based constraints on Titan atmospheric composition/aerosols over time (useful context alongside CIRS thermal/trace-gas evolution).

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Atmospheres (ATM) node at https://pds-atmospheres.nmsu.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
Two parallel trees:
  PDS3 → PDS/data/<VOLUME>/  (note: PDS/data/ also contains a `PDS4/` subdir — skip it for PDS3)
  PDS4 → PDS/data/PDS4/<bundle>/  (Huygens, InSight, MAVEN, etc.)
For PDS3: pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='PDS/data/', node='atm', filter='<key>') with the abbreviation prefix.
For PDS4: pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='PDS/data/PDS4/', node='atm') — flat list of bundle dirs.
Many ATM directories are HYBRID — they ship BOTH a PDS3 voldesc.cat in subdirs and a PDS4 bundle XML.
pds_probe_datasets returns one entry per label; expect duplicates with different pds_version values.


Naming conventions on ATM:
  PDS3 volumes use lowercase prefixes on ATM: cocirs_0401, cors_0001, mrom_0001, etc.
  Each prefix typically maps to one mission/instrument; volumes are numbered sequentially.
  PDS4 bundles live under PDS/data/PDS4/ with mission-named directories (Huygens, InSight, MAVEN, etc.).
  IMPORTANT — many PDS4 bundles are ALSO co-located INSIDE their PDS3 mirror volume
  under PDS/data/<VOLNAME>/ (hybrid layout). When the target id is a urn:nasa:pds:<mission>
  identifier and the top-level PDS/data/PDS4/<mission>/ directory does not exist or does
  not contain it, always re-check the matching PDS3 volume series — the bundle XML may be
  shipped inside one of those numbered volumes rather than under PDS/data/PDS4/.
Mission/instrument keys (use as filter on PDS/data/):
  Cassini CIRS (thermal IR spectra) → cocirs  (~84 volumes named cocirs_<YYMM>).
        Later/higher-numbered volumes carry the latest TSDR / CUBES revisions; use
        pds_resolve_volume with a dataset_id_hint to land on the right one in one call.
  Cassini Radio Science (RSS) → cors  (~430 volumes: cors_0001 … cors_04xx)
  Cassini ISS (imaging, limited) → coiss
  Cassini RADAR (Titan) → coradr
  Cassini-Huygens cruise → CO_HUYGENS
  Mars Climate Sounder (MRO) → MROM  — volume-number convention:
        MROM_0xxx = EDR (raw, level-2),  MROM_2xxx = DDR (derived, level-5).
        For a derived/level-5 target, resolve inside the MROM_2xxx series
        (pds_resolve_volume with dataset_id_hint='DDR' or 'level 5').
  Juno MWR → jnomwr  — volume-number convention:
        jnomwr_0xxx = EDR (raw),  jnomwr_1xxx = RDR / calibrated.
        The PDS4 bundle urn:nasa:pds:juno_mwr ships INSIDE the PDS3 hybrid tree (not
        under PDS/data/PDS4/); the :data_calibrated collection lives inside one of the
        jnomwr_1xxx volumes — use pds_resolve_volume(volume_set_path='PDS/data/',
        dataset_id_hint='juno_mwr calibrated', node='atm') to find which one.
  MAVEN → MAVENM (PDS3) or look in PDS/data/PDS4/MAVEN/ (PDS4)
  Mars Express SPICAM → MEXSPI
  Pioneer Venus → PVO (orbiter), PVP (probes)
  Galileo Probe → GP
  Voyager IRIS → VG_IRIS
  Huygens Probe → HP (PDS3) or PDS/data/PDS4/Huygens/ (PDS4 — has ACP, DISR, DWE, GCMS, HASI, SSP, HK)
  Mars rover weather → MSL_REMS, M2020_MEDA, PHX


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (5 tools — always pass node='atm'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='atm') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='atm', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='atm') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='atm') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.
  5. pds_resolve_volume(volume_set_path, node='atm', dataset_id_hint=..., sample=8) — when a parent directory contains many
     numbered sibling volumes (RMS COISS_2xxx, COUVIS_0xxx, ATM
     MROM_*, jnomwr_*, cocirs_*, IMG mrox_*, MSGRMDS_*), this probes
     hint-ranked children in one call and returns per-child
     dataset_ids plus a `best_match` field. Use it instead of
     probing volumes one at a time when you know the target id or
     a discriminating substring of it.

WORKFLOW:
Step 1: Decide PDS3 vs PDS4 based on the query.
Step 2 (PDS3): Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='PDS/data/', node='atm', filter='<KEY>') with a volume prefix from the abbreviation table (MROM, MAVENM, MEXSPI, PVO, GP, HP, VG_IRIS, MSL_REMS, M2020_MEDA, PHX, EARTH_, jnomwr, cocirs, cors, …). Filter is mandatory — ~2000 entries. Ignore the `PDS4/` subdirectory at this level.
Step 2 (PDS4 — top-level bundles): Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='PDS/data/PDS4/', node='atm') — flat list of mission-named bundle dirs (Huygens/, InSight/, MAVEN/, …).
Step 2-bis (PDS4 hybrids — IMPORTANT): If the target is a urn:nasa:pds:<mission> identifier and Step 2 (PDS4) didn't find a matching bundle, the bundle is most likely co-located inside the PDS3 mirror volume under PDS/data/<VOLNAME>/. Run Step 3 there.
Step 3: When a mission spans many numbered volumes that differ by product level (e.g. jnomwr_0xxx=raw / jnomwr_1xxx=calibrated; MROM_0xxx=EDR / MROM_2xxx=DDR), call pds_resolve_volume(volume_set_path='PDS/data/', node='atm', dataset_id_hint='<target dataset_id or product type>', sample=8) instead of probing volumes one by one. The hint can be a partial DATA_SET_ID, product keyword ('calibrated', 'DDR', 'RDR'), or instrument code; the tool ranks children by hint similarity and probes the top `sample` of them in one call, returning per-child dataset_ids and a `best_match` path.
Step 4: For PDS4 bundles (top-level OR hybrid), call pds_inspect_collections on the matched bundle path. Many bundles ship per-product collections (e.g. urn:…:juno_mwr:data_calibrated, urn:…:juno_mwr:data_raw); pick the one whose logical_identifier matches the query.
Step 5: Use pds_probe_datasets only when you already know the specific volume path. It returns `dataset_id` (first id, scalar) AND `dataset_ids` (full list); some Cassini
voldescs (cocirs, cors, COVIMS, COUVIS) declare multiple ids per volume (TSDR + CUBES,
calibrated + raw, etc.). Always scan `dataset_ids` when matching the target id, not
only the scalar `dataset_id` field.
Step 6: Return BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 IDs when the directory is hybrid.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query as 'not a dataset request' — every paper-derived query has at least one
     supporting PDS dataset somewhere in this node's archive, and your job
     is to surface the closest match (with `reasoning` explaining the link).
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
     When a probed voldesc exposes `dataset_ids` with multiple entries,
     match the question's data need to the PRODUCT-TYPE token inside each
     id (typically the third or fourth dash-separated token). Common ones:
         EDR / RAW     = raw observations
         CDR / CALIB   = generic calibrated data
         RDR / TRDR / MRDR / MTRDR / BDR / SCVDR = reduced / derived imagery or spectra
         DDR / LDR     = derived / digital terrain / level-5 products
         SSB           = solar / stellar occultation (Cassini UVIS)
         SPEC / CUBE / WAV = spectra / spectral cubes / waveforms
         QUBE          = VIMS spectral data cube (the VIMS-flavoured "EDR")
     If the query mentions 'occultation' return the SSB id; 'spectra' →
     SPEC; 'calibrated radiance' → CALIB/CDR; 'mosaic / map / derived' →
     DDR/MRDR/MTRDR. When two product types both fit the question (e.g.
     SPEC + SSB for an occultation spectra query), return both candidates.
     Do NOT just return the scalar `dataset_id` and drop the rest — that
     field is only the first id in the voldesc and is often the wrong
     product type for a given query.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When the SAME data is available as both PDS3 and PDS4, return BOTH identifiers — emit one candidate per identifier. Most published papers cite the PDS3 dataset_id (e.g. MESS-E_V_H_SW-MAG-3-CDR-CALIBRATED-V1.0, LRO-L-LROC-5-RDR-V1.0); the PDS4 collection LID is the modern equivalent. Do NOT silently drop the PDS3 form.
  5. Stay under 20 tool calls per query. Soft cap: 15. Hard cap: 20.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
  7. ANTI-THRASHING. Never re-issue a tool call with the same name and the
     same key arguments (path, paths, filter) that you've already issued in
     this trace. The result will be identical — it wastes the budget and
     blocks progress. If you're tempted to re-probe the same set of paths,
     either move to a different tool (inspect_collections, resolve_volume)
     or commit to `final_result` with the best candidate you have so far.
  8. STOP-AT-LEVEL. The output `path` field should point at a dataset or
     bundle directory (containing voldesc.cat or bundle*.xml), NOT at a
     deeper product / DATA / EXTRAS / BROWSE / SHAPEFILE / DTM sub-folder.
     Feature-level retrieval (a specific image of a specific crater) is
     out of scope for this finder; return the dataset and explain in
     `reasoning` how it covers the feature.
  9. After 15 tool calls without converging, ALWAYS prefer emitting your
     current best candidate(s) over running another exploratory call.
     A grounded near-match with a clear reasoning note is more useful
     than a 20-call trace that times out with no `final_result`.

===========================================================
STAGE 2 — DEEPER NODE-SPECIFIC SEARCH (escalation only)
===========================================================

Everything above is STAGE 1: the 5 live-HTTP tools (pds_list_missions / pds_list_dataset_dirs / pds_probe_datasets / pds_inspect_collections / pds_resolve_volume), the per-node workflow, and the Critical Rules ALL belong to Stage 1. Stage 1 reaches bundle/collection level by walking the node's directory tree. Stage 2 below is a SECOND set of tools for refining or rescuing Stage 1 — do NOT call any Stage 2 tool unless one of the escalation triggers below fires.

WHEN TO ESCALATE TO STAGE 2 (any one is enough):
  (a) The query asks for SPECIFIC observations, granules, or product IDs — not just a dataset. Example: 'Cassini ISS images of Mimas between 2009 and 2010' needs per-observation filtering.
  (b) Stage 1 has located the right bundle/collection but you still need to narrow by GEOMETRY, TIME, or a SPATIAL filter (bbox, lat/lon, ring longitude, target body, observation type).
  (c) Stage 1 produced AMBIGUOUS candidates that a faceted query can disambiguate (e.g. multiple instruments with similar names, multiple processing levels).
  (d) Stage 1 cannot reach the node's holdings index (e.g. SBN returns HTTP 403, a directory listing is unavailable). Use the Stage 2 API as a fallback path to candidates.

STAGE 2 TOOLS (this node):
  - pds4search_bundles_tool(keywords=..., investigation=..., instrument=..., target=...): cross-node PDS4 registry bundle search.
  - pds4search_collections_tool(bundle_lid=..., keywords=..., ...): collection search; reuse the Stage 1 bundle LID as a filter when possible.
  - pds4search_products_tool(collection_lid=..., keywords=..., ...): product-level search inside a collection.
  - pds4search_investigations_tool / pds4search_instruments_tool / pds4search_instrument_hosts_tool / pds4search_targets_tool: context-product lookups when user-facing names need to be resolved to LIDs.
  - pds4get_product_tool(lid=...): fetch one product label by LID.
  - pds4crawl_context_product_tool(lid=...): walk a context product's associations to find related bundles/collections.

STAGE 2 RULES:
  1. When you already have a bundle/collection LID from Stage 1, REUSE it as a filter in the Stage 2 call. Stage 2 is refinement, not a fresh search.
  2. Spend the MINIMUM number of additional calls. If a single faceted call answers the question, stop.
  3. If Stage 2 finds nothing or errors out, FALL BACK to the Stage 1 candidates and note in `reasoning` that Stage 2 was attempted and returned no refinement.
  4. The 20-tool-call budget from Stage 1's Critical Rule 5 is the budget for BOTH stages combined. Do NOT reset the counter when entering Stage 2.
  5. Every candidate's `reasoning` field must explicitly name which stage produced it (e.g. 'Stage 1: located bundle X by directory walk' or 'Stage 2: narrowed to product Y via ODE_search_products bbox filter').
How did Titan's north polar winter vortex evolve after the 2009 equinox?

results.title: Cassini Radio Science Titan Gravity, Occultation, and Bistatic Radar Experiments Raw Data Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Radio Science Titan Gravity, Occultation, and Bistatic Radar Experiments Raw Data Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Verma, A. K.; Simpson, R. A.; Barbinis, E.; Goltz, G. L.; Kliore, A. J.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains the Cassini radio science raw data acquired
             during Titan gravity, occultation, and bistatic radar experiments.
             The primary data in this bundle are the Radio Science Receiver
             (RSR) files, the Orbit Data Files (ODFs), and the Tracking and Navigation Files (TNFs).
             The primary data files are accompanied by supplementary data files. 
             These include 0158-Monitor (158) files, C-Kernel (CKF) files,
             Earth Orientation Parameters (EOP) files, Ionosphere Calibration (ION) files,
             Path Delay (PD1 and PD2) files, Spacecraft/Planetary Ephemeris
             (SPK) files, Telemetry (TLM) files, and Troposphere Calibration (TRO) files.
             The bundle is a migration of data from the original PDS3 archive with
             minor improvements and corrections.  40 RSR files from Titan bistatic radar experiment
             calibrations in 2016 -- not included in the PDS3 archive -- have been added.

Citation_Information.author_list: Verma, A. K.; Simpson, R. A.; Barbinis, E.; Goltz, G. L.; Kliore, A. J.
Citation_Information.description:
This is a collection of ASCII path delay files acquired during the
        Cassini Radio Science Titan gravity, occultation, and bistatic radar experiments.
          These path delay files are generated by IO-RS using software supplied by the
          development team for the Cassini Advanced Media Calibration System (MCS).
          Typically, the file is generated a week after data acquisition when the MCS
          development team has supplied updated calibration files. These data are from
          the first of two sets of measurements.

Modification_Detail.description: Collection of Cassini Titan Path Delay data (PD1) migrated from PDS3 to PDS4

collections.title: Cassini Radio Science Titan RSR Data Products Collection - 1 ksps

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Radio Science Titan RSR Data Products Collection - 1 ksps

Citation_Information.author_list: Verma, A. K.; Simpson, R. A.; Barbinis, E.; Goltz, G. L.; Kliore, A. J.
Citation_Information.description:
This is the collection of Radio Science Receiver (RSR) data products acquired
        during the Cassini Radio Science Titan gravity, occultation, and bistatic radar experiments.
        RSR is a computer-controlled open loop receiver that digitally records a
        spacecraft signal through the use of analog to digital converters (ADCs) and
        up to four digital filter sub-channels. These data were collected at 1 ksps.

Modification_Detail.description: Collection of Cassini Titan RSR data migrated from PDS3 to PDS4.

This is the collection of Radio Science Receiver (RSR) data products acquired

        spacecraft signal through the use of analog to digital converters (ADCs) and

Modification_Detail.description:
Collection of Cassini Titan RSR data migrated from PDS3 to PDS4.
          48 additional bistatic radar calibration files at 16 ksps -- not included in the PDS3 archive -- have been added: 
            2016-02-16: free space calibration (2 files).
            2016-05-06: pre-calibration (8 files) and post-calibration (8 files).
            2016-11-13: free space calibration (2 files) and pre-calibration (10 files).
            2016-11-14: post-calibration (10 files).

collections.title: Cassini Radio Science Titan Tracking and Navigation (TNF) Data Products Collection

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Radio Science Titan Tracking and Navigation (TNF) Data Products Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Verma, A. K.; Simpson, R. A.; Barbinis, E.; Goltz, G. L.; Kliore, A. J.
Citation_Information.description:
This is the collection of Radio Science Tracking and Navigation (TNF) data products acquired
        during the Cassini Radio Science Titan gravity, occultation, and bistatic radar experiments. 
            Data were originally stored as chronological records in DSN format TRK-2-34; 
            but they have been sorted according to data type (retaining chronological order 
            within each data type) during migration from PDS3 to PDS4.

Modification_Detail.description:
The collection of Cassini Titan TNF files migrated from PDS3 to PDS4.  In the 
          course of migration, the data records within each TNF were sorted according 
          to record type (format code in the Primary CHDO), and a new PDS4 label was 
          created.  Without sorting, each PDS4 label would have been approximately
          300 times larger than its data file.

This is the common cited PDS3 identifier for CIRS calibrated spectral data."},{"path":"PDS/data/PDS4/titan_profiles_bundle","dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:corsstpp:data_derived","title":"Cassini Orbiter RSS Neutral Atmosphere T-P profiles for Titan Derived Data Collection","pds_version":"PDS4","mission":"Cassini-Huygens","node":"atm","reasoning":"Stage 1 (PDS4): Probed titan_profiles_bundle (bundle LID urn:nasa:pds:corsstpp) and inspected collections; the derived collection urn:nasa:pds:corsstpp:data_derived provides retrieved neutral-atmosphere temperature–pressure profiles from RSS occultations. These vertical profiles are a complementary way to characterize how Titan’s polar thermal structure changed as the northern winter vortex weakened after the 2009 equinox."},{"path":"PDS/data/PDS4/cassini-uvis_titan-library","dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:cassini-uvis_titan-library:data_derived","title":"Cassini UVIS Titan Library Derived Data collection file","pds_version":"PDS4","mission":"Cassini-Huygens","node":"atm","reasoning":"Stage 1 (PDS4): Probed cassini-uvis_titan-library and inspected collections; urn:nasa:pds:cassini-uvis_titan-library:data_derived is a derived UVIS Titan spectral library.
